tasks.description: Install AI SDK packages on the backend so the chat endpoint can stream in Data Stream Protocol format.
steps: Run `cd apps/api && pnpm add ai @ai-sdk/anthropic`
Run `pnpm install` from root to sync lockfile
Verify packages resolve: `cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5`
tasks.verify: cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Install AI SDK and React hooks on the frontend for useChat() integration.
steps: Run `cd apps/web && pnpm add ai @ai-sdk/react`
Run `pnpm install` from root to sync lockfile
Verify packages resolve: `cd apps/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5`
tasks.verify: cd apps/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Create a Mastra-to-AI-SDK tool adapter so existing ToolRegistryService output works with streamText().
steps: Create `apps/api/src/agents/chat/tool-adapter.ts`
Import `tool` from `ai` and `ToolsInput` from `@mastra/core/agent`
Export `toAISDKTools(mastraTools: ToolsInput)` that iterates entries and returns `Record<string, CoreTool>`
For each Mastra tool: map `description` → `description`, `inputSchema` → `parameters`
For execute: verify Mastra's execute signature vs AI SDK's `({ ...args }) => ...` — wrap if needed
Handle empty tools gracefully (return `{}`)
tasks.verify: cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Create the chat DTO to validate incoming useChat() message payloads. Must handle forbidNonWhitelisted global pipe.
steps: Create `apps/api/src/agents/chat/agent-chat.dto.ts`
Define `AgentChatDto` with `@IsArray() messages: any[]` — let AI SDK handle message validation internally
Do NOT add nested DTO validation on individual messages — useChat sends `{ id, role, content, createdAt }` which would need `@Allow()` on every field
Optionally add `@Allow()` catch-all or `@IsOptional() data?: any` in case useChat sends extra fields
tasks.verify: cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Create the chat service that loads agent config from DB, builds tools, resolves the AI SDK model, and streams via streamText().
steps: Create `apps/api/src/agents/chat/agent-chat.service.ts`
Inject `AgentsService` (from AgentsModule) and `ToolRegistryService` (from ToolsModule)
Implement `streamChat(agentId: string, messages: CoreMessage[], userId: string)`:
1. Load agent via `agentsService.getAgent(agentId)` (throws NotFoundException if missing)
2. Build tools via `toolRegistry.buildToolsForAgent(agentId, userId)`, convert with `toAISDKTools()`
3. Resolve model: `createAnthropic()((agent.llmModel || 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6').replace('anthropic/', ''))`
4. Call `streamText({ model, system: agent.instructions, messages, tools, maxSteps: 5 })`
5. Return the streamText result object
tasks.verify: cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Create the chat controller using @Res() and pipeDataStreamToResponse() to stream AI SDK format to the client.
steps: Create `apps/api/src/agents/chat/agent-chat.controller.ts`
Use `@Controller('agents')` (matches existing pattern with AgentPreviewController)
Define `@Post(':id/chat')` endpoint
Inject auth via `@Session() session: UserSession`
Parse body via `@Body() dto: AgentChatDto`
Use `@Res() res: Response` to access the Express response directly
Call `chatService.streamChat(id, dto.messages, session.user.id)`
Pipe result to response via `result.pipeDataStreamToResponse(res)` from `ai` package
NOTE: when using `@Res()`, NestJS bypasses its own response handling — this is correct for streaming
tasks.verify: cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Create the chat NestJS module and register it in app.module.ts alongside AgentPreviewModule.
steps: Create `apps/api/src/agents/chat/agent-chat.module.ts`
Import `AgentsModule` (provides AgentsService) and `ToolsModule` (provides ToolRegistryService)
MastraModule and ActivityModule are @Global() — no import needed
Register controller: `AgentChatController`, provider: `AgentChatService`
Add `AgentChatModule` to `imports` array in `apps/api/src/app.module.ts` (next to `AgentPreviewModule`)
tasks.verify: cd apps/api && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Build the ChatMessage component that renders user messages, assistant text, and tool invocations with a minimal agentic feel.
steps: Create `apps/web/src/components/agents/chat/chat-message.tsx`
Accept a single AI SDK `Message` as prop (import from `@ai-sdk/react`)
User messages: right-aligned, cyan-tinted bubble (match `step-test-run.tsx` style: `bg-cyan-400/15`)
Assistant messages: left-aligned, secondary bg, whitespace-pre-wrap for formatting
Tool invocations (`message.toolInvocations`): render inline between text parts
- State 'call': show tool name + args in a compact card with a spinner
- State 'result': show tool name + result summary (truncated) with a check icon
- Use monospace font for tool names, subtle `border-border` styling
- Make tool args/results collapsible via a simple toggle (no heavy accordion — just a chevron)
Empty assistant message + loading: show bounce dots animation (reuse pattern from step-test-run.tsx)
tasks.verify: cd apps/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Build the ChatPanel component wrapping useChat() with cross-origin credential support and auto-scroll.
steps: Create `apps/web/src/components/agents/chat/chat-panel.tsx`
Props: `agentId: string`, `agentName: string`
Read API URL: `const API_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:4200'`
Use `useChat({ api: `${API_URL}/agents/${agentId}/chat`, fetch: (url, init) => fetch(url, { ...init, credentials: 'include' }) })`
Destructure: `{ messages, input, handleInputChange, handleSubmit, isLoading, error }`
Render: header strip with agent name + 'Preview' badge, scrollable message list, input bar
Auto-scroll: useRef on scroll container, useEffect on messages.length to scrollTo bottom
Input: form with text input + ArrowUp send button, disabled when isLoading
Error: show subtle error banner if `error` is set
Empty state: centered text 'Send a message to chat with {agentName}'
tasks.verify: cd apps/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5

tasks.description: Integrate ChatPanel into the agent detail page as a sticky side panel, replacing the Chat button redirect.
steps: Modify `apps/web/src/app/(app)/agents/[id]/page.tsx`
Change outer container from `max-w-2xl` single column to a two-column flex/grid layout:
- Left column: existing config sections (scrollable, ~60% width, max-w-2xl)
- Right column: ChatPanel (sticky, full viewport height, ~40% width, min-w ~380px)
Replace the 'Chat' button (currently routes to /live) with a 'Voice' button to /live. The chat is now inline.
Import and render `<ChatPanel agentId={id} agentName={agent.name} />`
On small screens (<lg), stack vertically or hide chat panel behind a toggle button
tasks.verify: cd apps/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5
